key_insight: SecureCROWN shows that neural network robustness certification can be done privately, revealing only the final verification result while protecting both model and data.
engineering_relevance: This makes robustness verification practical for privacy-sensitive and IP-sensitive deployments such as healthcare, finance, and proprietary ML services.
method: Built a privacy-preserving two-party computation (2PC) protocol for certified robustness verification.
Replaced data-dependent branching in Linear Bound Propagation with continuous arithmetic formulations to make secure computation feasible.
Added Newton--Raphson refinement to improve numerical stability and maintain plaintext-equivalent bounds.
evidence_signal: Experiments show SecureCROWN matches plaintext verification exactly and runs in about 0.1 to 200 seconds across different model sizes and LAN/WAN settings.
watch_item: The approach relies on the semi-honest security model and may incur communication/latency overhead in larger or more adversarial deployments.
paper_title: Privacy-Preserving Robustness Verification for Neural Networks
